_meta.description: EEG sample registry — tagged datasets for BCI security research and testing
_meta.schema: Each entry: id, name, source, sourceUrl, type, condition[], dsm5Code, channels, samplingRateHz, format, license, redistributable, subjects, taraId, paradigm, notes

samples.name: ADHD Adult Resting State + Cognitive
samples.subjectBreakdown: 37 ADHD + 42 healthy controls, ages 20-68, 56M/23F
samples.paradigm: Eyes open, eyes closed, cognitive challenge, sound listening
samples.taraRelevance: Frontal theta elevation at Fz illustrates signal-level characteristics that a signal injection attack would produce (for threat modeling purposes). Note: theta/beta ratio alone is insufficient for ADHD diagnosis per AAN practice advisory (2016, PMID 27760867)
samples.notes: CC BY 4.0 — freely redistributable with attribution. Subject 7 female ADHD group has corrupted signal. Published by Nasrabadi et al.

samples.name: ADHD/Control Children EEG
samples.subjectBreakdown: 61 ADHD + 60 controls, ages 7-12, boys and girls
samples.paradigm: Resting state, 10-20 standard montage
samples.taraRelevance: Pediatric ADHD theta/beta ratio at frontal electrodes demonstrates what abnormal slow-wave injection would produce (for threat modeling purposes). Note: theta/beta ratio alone is insufficient for ADHD diagnosis per AAN practice advisory (2016, PMID 27760867)
samples.notes: IEEE DataPort Open Access — requires IEEE account, no redistribution rights. DSM-IV diagnosed, Ritalin-treated up to 6 months.

samples.name: FOCUS: ADHD EEG During Gameplay
samples.subjectBreakdown: ADHD and non-ADHD adults during brain-controlled game
samples.paradigm: Active cognitive gameplay with EMOTIV Epoc
samples.notes: IEEE DataPort Open Access. DOI: 10.21227/0wa8-0b66. Alchalabi, Shirmohammadi, Eddin (2020).

samples.name: CHB-MIT Scalp EEG (Epilepsy)
samples.subjectBreakdown: 22 pediatric (23 cases), 5M ages 3-22, 17F ages 1.5-19. 664 EDF files, 198 seizure events.
samples.paradigm: Continuous monitoring, post-medication-withdrawal, seizure annotations in .seizures files
samples.taraRelevance: Ictal onset patterns (high-amplitude delta, spike-wave complexes) represent what neuronal jamming would produce (for threat modeling purposes)
samples.notes: Children's Hospital Boston / MIT. ODC-BY — freely redistributable. Gold standard for seizure detection research.

samples.name: Sleep-EDF Database Expanded
samples.subjectBreakdown: SC: 153 healthy adults 25-101 (1987-91). ST: 44 recordings, 22 subjects, temazepam study (1994).
samples.paradigm: Whole-night PSG, sleep stage labels: W, N1, N2, N3, REM
samples.taraRelevance: Sleep stage transitions show natural band power shifts; noise masking attacks would disrupt these detectable transitions (for threat modeling purposes)
samples.notes: ODC-BY — freely redistributable. Standard benchmark for sleep staging algorithms.

samples.name: PhysioNet EEG Motor Movement/Imagery
samples.subjectBreakdown: 109 volunteers, 1500+ recordings, BCI2000 system
samples.paradigm: Real and imagined movement: left fist, right fist, both fists, both feet + baseline
samples.taraRelevance: Motor imagery ERD (mu rhythm suppression at C3/C4) is the signal a replay attack would capture; resting alpha is the RF entrainment target (for threat modeling purposes)
samples.notes: ODC-BY — freely redistributable. Already integrated in qif-lab/src/real_data.py via download_eegbci().

samples.name: BCI Competition IV Dataset 2a (4-Class)
samples.subjectBreakdown: 9 subjects, 2 sessions each, 288 trials/session
samples.paradigm: 4-class motor imagery: left hand, right hand, feet, tongue
samples.notes: Academic citation required. Available via MOABB as BNCI2014_001.

samples.name: UCI EEG Database (Alcoholism)
samples.subjectBreakdown: Alcoholic (genetic predisposition) vs. control, visual stimulus paradigm
samples.paradigm: Visual stimulus matching: 1-stimulus, match, no-match conditions
samples.taraRelevance: Chronic exposure effects on ERP morphology illustrate long-term neural signal degradation patterns (for threat modeling purposes)
samples.notes: CC BY 4.0 — freely redistributable. Often confused with ADHD in secondary sources; this is alcoholism data.

samples.name: DEAP Emotion Dataset
samples.subjectBreakdown: 32 participants, 40 one-minute music video clips
samples.paradigm: Arousal, valence, dominance, liking — continuous self-report
samples.notes: Academic only — requires application with institutional email. Koelstra et al. IEEE TAFFC 2012.

samples.name: SEED Emotion EEG Dataset
samples.subjectBreakdown: 15 subjects x 3 sessions (~1 week apart), 15 film clips/session
samples.paradigm: Positive, negative, neutral emotion elicited by film stimuli
samples.notes: Academic registration required. Differential entropy features pre-computed. Variants: SEED-IV, SEED-V, SEED-VIG.

samples.name: QIF-Lab Healthy Baseline (Synthetic)
samples.subjectBreakdown: Generated by qif-lab/src/synthetic_data.py
samples.paradigm: Mathematically synthesized multi-band EEG (delta, theta, alpha, beta, gamma)
samples.notes: QIF-internal synthetic. BrainFlow SYNTHETIC_BOARD or custom generator. 8 scenarios available: healthy_baseline, noisy_but_safe, degraded_signal, signal_injection_attack, phase_disruption_attack, amplitude_spike_attack, quantum_regime_short, decoherence_boundary.

samples.name: QIF-Lab BrainFlow Synthetic Board
samples.subjectBreakdown: Generated in real-time by BrainFlow SYNTHETIC_BOARD
samples.paradigm: Realistic multi-band spectral content, no clinical meaning
samples.notes: BrainFlow SDK synthetic board. No pre-recorded files — generates data on demand. Used in test_brainflow.py and synthetic_data.py.

samples.name: Neurowall SSVEP Attack Scenario
samples.subjectBreakdown: Generated by tools/neurowall/sim.py
samples.paradigm: Clean EEG + injected SSVEP at 15Hz and 10.9Hz, processed through 3-layer Neurowall pipeline
samples.notes: QIF-internal. Tests L1 notch filter, L2 differential privacy, L3 NISS policy engine. Paired with neurowall ROC data (roc_data.json).

samples.name: Neurosim Signal Injection Attacks
samples.subjectBreakdown: Generated by tools/neurosim/qif-attack-simulator/
samples.paradigm: Multiple TARA-mapped attack techniques: SSVEP injection, impedance manipulation, neuronal flooding, signal replay
samples.notes: QIF-internal. Full attack simulator with registry.py mapping to TARA catalog. Includes signal_injection.py, evasion.py, feedback.py.

samples.name: Neurosim Evasion Attacks
samples.subjectBreakdown: Generated by tools/neurosim/qif-attack-simulator/attacks/evasion.py
samples.paradigm: Slow-onset evasion techniques designed to bypass detection thresholds
samples.notes: QIF-internal. DC drift, boiling frog (gradual amplitude shift), envelope modulation.

samples.name: Neurosim Closed-Loop Cascade Attack
samples.subjectBreakdown: Generated by tools/neurosim/qif-attack-simulator/attacks/feedback.py
samples.paradigm: Closed-loop feedback attack exploiting BCI decode-stimulate cycle
samples.notes: QIF-internal. Most dangerous attack class — self-amplifying through neurofeedback loop.

samples.name: ASD Brain Aging Resting-State EEG (Dickinson et al.)
samples.subjectBreakdown: 28 ASD + 28 neurotypical controls, ages 18-68, Biosemi ActiveTwo
samples.paradigm: 2.5 min eyes-closed resting state
samples.taraRelevance: Peak alpha frequency mapping, oscillatory slowing analysis — establishes adult ASD baseline for TARA frequency-band calibration
samples.notes: CC BY 4.0. Downloaded to eeg-data-lake/raw/dickinson-brain-aging/. Citation: Dickinson, A., Jeste, S., & Milne, E. (2022). Cortex, 148, 139-151.

samples.name: BCIAUT-P300: ASD P300 BCI Multi-Session
samples.subjectBreakdown: 15 ASD subjects, 7 sessions each (105 total sessions), ages 16-38, g.Nautilus wireless
samples.paradigm: P300 oddball BCI joint-attention training
samples.taraRelevance: P300 ERP latency/amplitude analysis in ASD — calibrates ERP-targeting TARA techniques
samples.notes: CC BY 4.0. 5.9 GB. Citation: Simoes, M. et al. (2020). Frontiers in Neuroscience, 14, 568104.

samples.name: SFARI EEG Multi-Paradigm Dataset
samples.subjectBreakdown: SFARI ASD biomarker cohort — verify subject counts on OpenNeuro
samples.paradigm: Multi-paradigm (resting + task, details on dataset page)
samples.taraRelevance: Cross-validation of ASD EEG biomarkers against HBN findings
samples.notes: CC0 — public domain. Verify metadata directly on OpenNeuro page.

samples.name: Move4AS: ASD Motor Function + Motion Capture
samples.subjectBreakdown: 14 ASD + 20 neurotypical controls, 16-channel wireless EEG + 3D motion capture
samples.paradigm: Motor imitation tasks: dancing and walking paradigms
samples.taraRelevance: Motor-related EEG in ASD — mu rhythm suppression analysis for sensorimotor TARA techniques
samples.notes: CC BY 4.0. Citation: Move4AS (2025). Scientific Data.

samples.name: Healthy Brain Network EEG (ASD + ADHD + TD)
samples.subjectBreakdown: 3000+ pediatric (66 ASD, ~406 ADHD, + other NDD + TD), ages 5-21, 128-channel EGI
samples.paradigm: Resting state, face processing, auditory processing, video viewing, cognitive battery
samples.taraRelevance: Largest pediatric ASD+ADHD EEG. Multi-paradigm for TARA baseline definition across 5-21 age range. Alpha/gamma/coherence/N170/microstates
samples.notes: RESTRICTED — requires signed DUA with Child Mind Institute. Cannot redistribute. Must notify CMI before publishing. Email: CMIDataUsage@childmind.org

samples.name: ABC-CT: Autism Biomarkers Consortium EEG
samples.subjectBreakdown: 280 ASD + 119 TD controls, ages 6-11, 5 multi-sites, 128-channel EGI, ADOS-2 confirmed
samples.paradigm: Resting state, face perception, biological motion, VEP
samples.taraRelevance: Gold-standard ASD EEG biomarker study. N170 face ERP accepted into FDA Biomarker Qualification Program. Directly calibrates face-processing TARA techniques
samples.notes: RESTRICTED — requires NDA Data Use Certification. Apply at nda.nih.gov. Citation: Jeste et al. (2022). AJP, 179(9), 656-668.

samples.name: Simons Sleep Project: ASD Sleep EEG
samples.subjectBreakdown: 102 ASD + 98 non-ASD siblings, ages 10-17, Dreem3 headband, 3600+ nights
samples.paradigm: Home-based longitudinal sleep EEG
samples.taraRelevance: ASD sleep architecture: slow-wave activity, spindle morphology, sleep staging for TARA low-frequency band calibration
samples.notes: RESTRICTED — requires SFARI Base application + IRB approval + Researcher Distribution Agreement.

samples.name: TDBRAIN: Two Decades Brainclinics Research Archive
samples.subjectBreakdown: 271 ADHD + 426 MDD + 75 OCD + 119 SMC + others, ages 5-89, 26-channel clinical EEG
samples.paradigm: 2 min eyes-open + 2 min eyes-closed resting state
samples.taraRelevance: Largest clinical ADHD EEG archive. Lifespan TBR analysis for TARA theta/beta frequency-band baseline
samples.notes: ORCID-gated access at brainclinics.com. Citation: de Godoy et al. (2022). Scientific Data, 9, 333.

samples.name: qEEG Children ADHD (Duric et al.)
samples.subjectBreakdown: 89 ADHD + 77 healthy controls, ages 6-15, ICD-10 diagnosed
samples.paradigm: qEEG spectral analysis, eyes-closed and eyes-open resting state
samples.taraRelevance: Pediatric ADHD qEEG features for age-dependent TBR analysis
samples.notes: CC BY 4.0. Note: this is processed qEEG feature data (tabular), not raw EEG signals. Citation: Duric et al. (2023). Applied Neuropsychology: Child.

samples.name: IDD and Healthy Controls EEG
samples.subjectBreakdown: 7 IDD + 7 TD controls, EMOTIV EPOC+ consumer-grade
samples.paradigm: 2 min resting + 2 min music listening
samples.taraRelevance: Exploratory only — small sample, consumer hardware, IDD is broader than ASD
samples.notes: CC BY 4.0. Note: IDD is broader than ASD — this is intellectual/developmental disorder, not ASD-specific.
